title: Swedish Market Study: Employee Benefit Platform
subtitle: Exploring employer appetite for a multi-benefit one-stop-shop — and what it means for long-term benefit strategy
challenge: During a period of significant global organizational change, the company set out to explore the Swedish market with a fresh lens. The core question: was there genuine employer appetite for a comprehensive, multi-benefit one-stop-shop platform — and if so, where, and why? Beyond assessing current demand, the study needed to surface deeper insights into how companies were thinking about benefits as a long-term strategic priority. The research would need to anticipate how employer needs and benefit management practices were likely to evolve, giving the organization a foundation to act on — not just react to.

designProcess.intro: Johan owned the study from end to end — planning the research approach, leading the team, managing stakeholder communications throughout, and delivering the final insights report. The work ran in parallel with ongoing organizational change, which meant maintaining clarity of purpose while navigating a fluid internal environment.

steps.title: Study Design & Stakeholder Alignment
steps.description: Defined the research scope and objectives in close collaboration with the stakeholder team. Surfaced internal assumptions about the Swedish market, benefit trends, and target employer segments. Coached colleagues on research methodology and established a shared framework for what success looked like before fieldwork began.

steps.title: Recruitment & User Interviews
steps.description: Oversaw the full recruitment process, designing criteria to ensure a diverse and representative sample of Swedish employers. Organized and conducted in-depth interviews with HR decision-makers and benefit managers across a range of company sizes and industries. Ongoing stakeholder communication kept the team informed and enabled real-time adjustment of research focus as patterns emerged.

steps.title: Analysis & Insight Delivery
steps.description: Ran a comprehensive analysis of interview findings, identifying patterns in appetite, hesitation, and unmet needs across employer segments. Synthesized the research into an actionable insights report that went beyond describing current attitudes — framing implications for how companies are likely to prioritize and manage benefits in the future, and translating this directly into GTM strategy recommendations.

insights: Employer appetite for platform consolidation was real but conditional — interest rose sharply when the platform was framed as reducing administrative complexity rather than adding a new tool

Benefit strategy was rarely proactive: most HR managers were reacting to employee requests rather than building benefit packages with long-term intent, creating an opening for a more strategic platform narrative

Trust and data sensitivity were recurring concerns, particularly among mid-sized employers evaluating a single platform for multiple benefit types — integration reassurances were needed earlier in the sales conversation

Future-facing needs pointed toward flexibility and personalization: employers anticipated increasing pressure to tailor benefits to diverse workforce segments, a gap the current market was not adequately addressing

images.alt: Research synthesis session mapping employer appetite across segments

deliverables.intro: The primary output was a comprehensive insights report delivered to the stakeholder team — structured to be both analytically rigorous and immediately actionable. The report covered segment-level findings on platform appetite, an assessment of adoption barriers, and forward-looking perspectives on how benefit management strategies were likely to evolve. Findings fed directly into go-to-market strategy discussions and helped the organization prioritize which employer segments to approach first.

images.alt: Segment opportunity mapping from the insights report
GTM strategy recommendations derived from employer interviews

results.intro: The study gave the organization something it hadn't had before entering the Swedish market: a grounded, evidence-based view of where employer appetite was real, where it wasn't, and what conditions would need to be true for the platform to succeed. Findings were delivered as actionable recommendations and used directly to inform go-to-market positioning.

kpis.label: Research scope
kpis.value: End-to-end ownership — from study design to final delivery

kpis.label: Primary output
kpis.value: Actionable insights report guiding GTM strategy

kpis.label: Strategic impact
kpis.value: Findings adopted into market entry and segment prioritization decisions
